api/spec/packages/legacy/src/productcata.purpose: TypeSpec source for v1 product catalog, subscription, and add-on API contracts. Defines all models, enums, unions, and route interfaces that compile into api/openapi.yaml and drive Go server stubs — never edit generated outputs, only these .tsp files.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none
patterns.description: Polymorphic types (Price, RateCard, RateCardEntitlement, SubscriptionEditOperation, SubscriptionCreate) use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" | "op" }) so the discriminator field sits at the top level with no wrapper.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Price { flat: FlatPrice, unit: UnitPrice, tiered: TieredPrice }

patterns.name: Visibility lifecycle annotation on every field
patterns.description: Every model field carries @visibility(Lifecycle.Read), @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create), or similar. Read-only computed fields (status, effectiveFrom, effectiveTo, version) are Lifecycle.Read only. Omit visibility only on structurally shared base models like RateCardBase<T>.

patterns.name: Spread-based model composition over inheritance
patterns.description: Models are composed by spreading shared base models (e.g. ...UniqueResource, ...ResourceTimestamps, ...RateCardBase<T>, ...SpendCommitments) rather than using TypeSpec extends, giving explicit field ownership and enabling OmitProperties<> overrides in create/update variants.
patterns.example: model RateCardFlatFee { ...RateCardBase<RateCardType.flatFee>; billingCadence: duration | null; price: FlatPriceWithPaymentTerm | null; }

patterns.name: Create/update variants via OmitProperties + withVisibility
patterns.description: Write models (PlanAddonCreate, SubscriptionAddonCreate) are produced with @withVisibility(Lifecycle.Create) and OmitProperties<DefaultKeyVisibility<Model, Lifecycle.Read>, "field">, not by defining duplicate models from scratch.

patterns.name: Route interfaces tagged with @tag("Product Catalog") or @tag("Subscriptions")
patterns.description: All route interfaces in routes.tsp use @route, @tag, @friendlyName, and per-operation @operationId + @summary. Operations return typed union responses (Model | NotFoundError | CommonErrors), never raw status codes alone.
patterns.example: @route("/api/v1/plans") @tag("Product Catalog") interface PlansEndpoints { @get @operationId("listPlans") list(...): PaginatedResponse<Plan> | CommonErrors; }

patterns.name: Shared error aliases from errors.tsp
patterns.description: Subscription routes use CommonSubscriptionErrors alias (defined in errors.tsp) that extends SubscriptionBadRequestErrorResponse and SubscriptionConflictErrorResponse with SubscriptionErrorExtensions. Feature/plan routes use CommonErrors from the parent package.

patterns.name: ISO8601 duration encoding for all duration fields
patterns.description: Every duration field uses @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) and an @example(duration.fromISO("P1M")) annotation. Null-able billing cadence (one-time fees) is typed as duration | null.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file that declares HTTP interfaces (FeaturesEndpoints, PlansEndpoints, PlanAddonsEndpoints, AddonsEndpoints, SubscriptionsEndpoints). Must import rest.tsp and errors.tsp; uses TypeSpec.Http and TypeSpec.OpenAPI.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new route interface without @operationId or @friendlyName breaks Go codegen naming. Missing 'using TypeSpec.Http;' causes @query/@route to fail.

key_file_guides.role: Defines RateCardBase<T>, RateCard union, RateCardFlatFee, RateCardUsageBased, RateCardUsageBasedPrice union, and entitlement templates. Central dependency imported by plan.tsp, addon.tsp, subscription.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new RateCard variant requires updating both the RateCard union and RateCardUsageBasedPrice union. Entitlement templates omit featureKey/featureId/usagePeriod via OmitProperties.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Price union and all price models (FlatPrice, UnitPrice, TieredPrice, DynamicPrice, PackagePrice) plus *WithCommitments variants. SpendCommitments is spread into usage-based price variants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New price type must be added to both the Price union and RateCardUsageBasedPrice union in ratecards.tsp. PriceType enum must also be extended.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Subscription, SubscriptionExpanded, SubscriptionPhase, SubscriptionItem, SubscriptionCreate/Change unions, SubscriptionEdit, SubscriptionEditOperation union, and SubscriptionTiming. Imports entitlements/main.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SubscriptionCreate and SubscriptionChange are @oneOf unions — new variants need explicit @summary. SubscriptionEditOperation uses discriminatorPropertyName: "op" not "type".

key_file_guides.role: Defines Plan, PlanPhase, PlanStatus, PlanOrderBy, PlanReferenceInput, SettlementMode. Plan.phases has @minItems(1). Imports prorating.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plan.validationErrors is typed ValidationError[] | null (not optional). New plan-level fields with Lifecycle.Create visibility must also appear in the ResourceCreateModel used by the create route.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Addon, AddonStatus (draft/active/archived), AddonInstanceType (single/multiple), AddonOrderBy. Mirrors Plan structure but without phases.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddonStatus lifecycle parallels PlanStatus but lacks 'scheduled'. Status is @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — never writable.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point — imports all sub-files in dependency order. New .tsp files must be imported here to be included in compilation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: features.tsp and subscriptionaddon.tsp are NOT imported here — features.tsp is used via the parent package, subscriptionaddon.tsp via routes.tsp import chain.

anti_patterns: Defining a new polymorphic type without @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", ... }) — omitting this produces a wrapped discriminator that breaks client deserialization.
Adding a route in any file other than routes.tsp — interfaces must be co-located there for @tag grouping and operationId consistency.
Duplicating model fields for create/update variants instead of using OmitProperties<DefaultKeyVisibility<Model, Lifecycle.Read>, "..."> with @withVisibility.
Using a duration field without @encode(DurationKnownEncoding.ISO8601) — the TypeSpec compiler defaults to seconds serialization, breaking ISO 8601 clients.
Hand-editing the generated api/openapi.yaml or api/api.gen.go — always regenerate via 'make gen-api' then 'make generate' after .tsp changes.

decisions.decision: Subscription edit operations use a separate 'op' discriminator (not 'type') in SubscriptionEditOperation union.
decisions.rationale: Distinguishes patch commands (add_item, remove_item, add_phase, etc.) from resource type discriminators used in Price/RateCard, preventing naming collisions in generated code and making PATCH semantics explicit.

decisions.decision: PlanAddonCreate and SubscriptionAddonCreate flatten the addonId into the body instead of embedding the full Addon.
decisions.rationale: On write operations only the add-on identifier is needed; embedding the full Addon object (which is read-only) would require clients to supply server-computed fields like status and effectiveFrom.

decisions.decision: CommonSubscriptionErrors is a local alias in errors.tsp rather than reusing CommonErrors from the parent.
decisions.rationale: Subscription mutations return SubscriptionBadRequestErrorResponse and SubscriptionConflictErrorResponse with SubscriptionErrorExtensions (validationErrors array) not present in the generic error types, requiring a domain-specific error alias.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new usage-based price type (e.g. 'stepped') to the product catalog
